title: `podman load` won't load a file with `:`
body:
### Issue Description

As the title says. I stumbled upon it, because many images have a colon in their name, so I tried to unpack an image to a file with a colon as well. But then executing a `podman load -i ./file_os:version` errors out.

Note: to simplify steps-to-reproduce I use an invalid OCI file, because that doesn't really matter in the context of the problem.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue *(in terms of terminal commands)*

```haskell
touch ubuntu:22.04.tar && podman load -i ./ubuntu:22.04.tar
```


### Describe the results you received

Complaints about a colon:

```haskell
Error: payload does not match any of the supported image formats:
 * oci: Invalid OCI reference: path ./ubuntu:22.04.tar contains a colon
 * oci-archive: Invalid OCI reference: path ./ubuntu:22.04.tar contains a colon
 * docker-archive: opening file "./ubuntu": open ./ubuntu: no such file or directory
 * dir: open ubuntu:22.04.tar/manifest.json: not a directory
```

### Describe the results you expected

Only complaints about the file being an invalid OCI archive, such as:

```
Error: payload does not match any of the supported image formats:
 * oci: parsing "localhost/./ubuntu_22.04.tar": parsing named reference "localhost/./ubuntu_22.04.tar": invalid reference format
 * oci-archive: loading index: open /var/tmp/oci1896190274/index.json: no such file or directory
 * docker-archive: loading tar component manifest.json: file does not exist
 * dir: open ubuntu_22.04.tar/manifest.json: not a directory
```

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @Hi-Angel !

Yes, colons in file paths are not supported as they are used for referencing images in multi-image archives (see https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-transports.5.md#ocipathreference).  While it's mentioned in `man containers-transports`, I think it wouldn't hurt to highlight that in the `podman-save` and `-load` man pages. Would that work for you?

> Thanks for reaching out, @Hi-Angel !
> 
> Yes, colons in file paths are not supported as they are used for referencing images in multi-image archives (see https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-transports.5.md#ocipathreference). While it's mentioned in `man containers-transports`, I think it wouldn't hurt to highlight that in the `podman-save` and `-load` man pages. Would that work for you?

I might be misunderstanding something, but isn't a user executing a `podman load -i …` is easily distinguishable compared to when podman loading functions are called internally? So one could either print an error referring to docs you mentioned, or even better to just load the file *(if it's possible)*.

I like your proposal.  My post-lunch brain didn't consider this option :^)
@mtrmac WDYT?

(The `:` being forbidden is already documented for `podman load`.)

---

What exactly is the proposal?

(c/image doesn’t have a good way to support `:` without problems, like `StringWithinTransport()` becoming ambiguous. I can’t immediately think of a local workaround; we would basically need a new transport name, `oci-archive-escaped:` or something like that.)

Well, at least it may be possible to process the `-i` command line argument of `podman load` to bail out with an explicit error that `:` is a special symbol used elsewhere in the internals and so needs to be avoided.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Please show the exact error you would like to see?

> Please show the exact error you would like to see?

Well, I think a `A colon is not allowed in the filename. Aborting…` might work. In case a colon is not the only character prohibited, a message like this may work: `The following characters are not allowed in a file name "…list…". Aborting…`. Or if you don't want to mention any specific characters at all *(which would be slightly worse usability, but figuring out "prohibited characters" most likely is an easy task anyway)*, this may work `Filename contains characters that are not allowed. Aborting…`.

Well the first error returned told you that.
So you want to go from:
```
$ touch ubuntu:22.04.tar && podman load -i ./ubuntu:22.04.tar
Error: payload does not match any of the supported image formats:
 * oci: Invalid OCI reference: path ./ubuntu:22.04.tar contains a colon
 * oci-archive: Invalid OCI reference: path ./ubuntu:22.04.tar contains a colon
 * docker-archive: opening file "./ubuntu": open ./ubuntu: no such file or directory
 * dir: open ubuntu:22.04.tar/manifest.json: not a directory
```
to
```
$ touch ubuntu:22.04.tar && podman load -i ./ubuntu:22.04.tar
oci: Invalid OCI reference: path ./ubuntu:22.04.tar contains a colon
The "oci: Invalid OCI reference" is just some internal detail that says nothing at all to a user. I think it's better to just `Error: path ./ubuntu:22.04.tar contains a colon`

The thing is we are trying not to put to much image name parsing in the client tool, since ib the past podman broke things allowed by the containers/image library. We also had situations where buildah/podamn and other tools disagreed on allowable image names.

Well… I don't the expertise to give any useful comment here… If you think improving the error message here will put a maintenance burden, then indeed it may be better to just close the issue. The message `podman` currently prints does have a small hint at the problem: it prints 4 different error messages, and 50% of them are actually valid ones. Not the best usability experience, granted… but sometimes "good enough" is better than "best".
